title: JIT IR translation — arithmetic, comparison, and control flow
purpose: Translate the core BEAM opcodes — data movement, arithmetic, comparison, and control flow — into Cranelift IR. These are the most frequently executed instructions and the highest-value JIT targets. Per D3 (tiered execution) and D5 (mixed-mode), unsupported opcodes trigger deoptimisation back to the interpreter rather than failing compilation.
task: Implement IR translation functions in two new files (ir_arithmetic.rs for data movement/arithmetic/comparison, ir_control.rs for call/return/branch/label). Each translator function takes a BEAM instruction and a Cranelift FunctionBuilder and emits the corresponding IR. The register file is modelled as a memory region (base pointer + offset). Arithmetic on tagged small integers extracts the payload, performs the native operation with overflow check, and re-tags the result. Overflow and unsupported opcodes emit deoptimisation exits to the interpreter. Scope-forward: ir_common.rs for shared helpers (register access, tag/untag, deopt exit builder) used by both files and future IR translation briefs (B-132, B-136).

requirements.title: Shared IR helpers — register access, tagging, deopt exits
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide helper functions for Cranelift IR emission that are shared across all opcode translators: register_load (emit IR to load a Term from x-register or y-register by index), register_store (emit IR to store a Term), untag_small_int (extract i64 payload from tagged Term), tag_small_int (re-tag i64 as Term), and emit_deopt_exit (emit a branch to an interpreter re-entry point). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT expose Cranelift types in these helpers' public signatures — they take and return beamr types.
acceptance: register_load(builder, reg_index, reg_kind) emits a Cranelift load from the register file memory region
register_store(builder, reg_index, reg_kind, value) emits a Cranelift store to the register file
untag_small_int(builder, tagged_value) emits a right-shift by TAG_BITS, returning an i64 IR value
tag_small_int(builder, raw_value) emits a left-shift by TAG_BITS OR'd with SMALL_INT_TAG
emit_deopt_exit(builder, instruction_index) emits a jump to an interpreter re-entry block with the current instruction pointer
Test: round-trip tag_small_int(untag_small_int(term)) == term for any small int

requirements.title: Data movement IR — move and swap
requirements.spec: WHEN the JIT compiler encounters a move instruction, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit Cranelift IR to load the source register and store to the destination register. WHEN the compiler encounters a swap instruction, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit IR to load both registers, then store each value to the other's location. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT modify the register file memory layout — it matches the interpreter's x-register and y-register arrays.
acceptance: translate_move(builder, source, dest) emits load+store IR for x/y registers
translate_swap(builder, reg_a, reg_b) emits load-load-store-store pattern (no temporary allocation)
move from literal (atom, small_int) emits an immediate constant load instead of a register load
Test: compiled move(x0, x1) with x0=42 produces x1=42
Test: compiled swap(x0, x1) with x0=1, x1=2 produces x0=2, x1=1

requirements.title: Arithmetic IR — add, subtract, multiply, div, rem with overflow deopt
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate arithmetic opcodes into Cranelift IR that: (1) untags both operands as small integers, (2) performs the native i64 operation with overflow detection, (3) re-tags the result. IF either operand is not a tagged small integer, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a deoptimisation exit to the interpreter. IF the native operation overflows, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a deoptimisation exit. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT implement bignum arithmetic — overflow deoptimises to the interpreter which handles bignums.
acceptance: translate_add(builder, lhs, rhs, dest) emits untag->iadd_checked->retag->store or deopt on overflow
translate_subtract(builder, lhs, rhs, dest) emits untag->isub_checked->retag->store or deopt
translate_multiply(builder, lhs, rhs, dest) emits untag->imul_checked->retag->store or deopt
translate_div(builder, lhs, rhs, dest) emits untag->sdiv->retag->store; division by zero deoptimises
translate_rem(builder, lhs, rhs, dest) emits untag->srem->retag->store; zero divisor deoptimises
Tag check on each operand emits conditional branch: if not small_int_tag then deopt exit
Test: compiled add(Term::small_int(2), Term::small_int(3)) returns Term::small_int(5)
Test: compiled add(SMALL_INT_MAX, 1) triggers deopt (overflow)

requirements.title: Comparison IR — relational and equality opcodes
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate comparison opcodes (is_lt, is_ge, is_eq, is_ne, is_eq_exact, is_ne_exact) into Cranelift IR that compares untagged small integer values and branches to the success or failure label. IF either operand is not a small integer, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a deoptimisation exit (full BEAM term ordering requires the atom table, which is not available in compiled code). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT implement the full BEAM term ordering in compiled code.
acceptance: translate_is_lt(builder, lhs, rhs, fail_label) emits icmp + conditional branch
translate_is_ge(builder, lhs, rhs, fail_label) emits icmp + conditional branch
translate_is_eq_exact(builder, lhs, rhs, fail_label) emits icmp_eq on raw tagged values (no untag needed for exact equality)
Non-small-int operands trigger deopt before comparison
Test: compiled is_lt(3, 5) falls through; is_lt(5, 3) branches to fail label

requirements.title: Control flow IR — labels, branches, and jumps
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate label instructions to Cranelift block headers. WHEN the compiler encounters a jump instruction, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a Cranelift jump to the corresponding block. WHEN the compiler encounters a conditional branch (e.g. from a test opcode), THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a Cranelift brif with correct true/false targets. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT create unreachable blocks — labels not targeted by any branch are still emitted but may be optimised away by Cranelift.
acceptance: translate_label(builder, label_id) starts a new Cranelift block keyed by label_id
translate_jump(builder, target_label) emits a Cranelift jump to the target block
A two-pass approach: first pass collects all labels into a HashMap<LabelId, Block>; second pass emits IR
Test: function with if/else branches executes the correct path based on input

requirements.title: Control flow IR — call, return, and inter-function dispatch
requirements.spec: WHEN the compiler encounters a call instruction to a function in the same module, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a direct Cranelift call if the callee is also compiled, or a call to jit_call_interpreted if not (per D5 mixed-mode). WHEN the compiler encounters a return instruction, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a Cranelift return with the value in x(0). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT implement tail call optimisation in this brief — all calls use standard call frames.
acceptance: translate_call(builder, target_mfa) emits either direct call or jit_call_interpreted helper call
translate_return(builder) emits Cranelift return with x(0) loaded as the return value
Call arguments are loaded from x-registers and passed as Cranelift function parameters
Return value is stored to x(0) after callee returns
Test: compiled function calling another compiled function returns the correct result
Test: compiled function calling an un-compiled function falls back to jit_call_interpreted

boundaries: SHALL NOT translate heap-allocating opcodes (put_tuple, put_list) — those need GC safepoints (B-134, B-136)
SHALL NOT implement the full BEAM term ordering in compiled code — non-small-int comparisons deoptimise
SHALL NOT implement tail call optimisation — standard call frames only in this brief
SHALL NOT implement bignum arithmetic — overflow deoptimises to the interpreter
SHALL NOT access the atom table from compiled code — atom operations deoptimise
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
Each ir_*.rs file is under 500 lines of production code
No Cranelift types appear in function signatures outside the jit/ module
